Ted's Health — Team Pages Dev Handoff

Generated: 28 June 2026 By: Nexus (AI Technical Lead) Phase: P1 content gap — doctor profiles

Bottom line

Yesterday's audit confirmed: Arc TRT has 4 individual clinician profile pages. Ted's Health has zero. This is a direct E-E-A-T and trust gap. Tonight I built 4 profile pages, a team hub, and a bonus metabolic health landing page. 6 production-ready files. Your dev team needs to host them and link them into the site.

Files delivered
teds-team-pages/index.html
→ tedshealth.com/team/
Team hub page. Lists all clinicians with mini bios and links to profiles. MedicalOrganization JSON-LD schema with employee list. This is the page to link to from the nav and About Us.
teds-team-pages/dr-jonathan-andrews.html
→ tedshealth.com/team/dr-jonathan-andrews/
Full profile: Medical Director. Bio, areas of expertise, trust signals, FAQs, CTAs. Physician JSON-LD schema. Target keywords: "TRT doctor UK", "testosterone specialist UK", "private testosterone doctor".
teds-team-pages/ryann-egang.html
→ tedshealth.com/team/ryann-egang/
Full profile: Registered Adult Nurse. Emphasises the continuity-of-care model and dual NHS/private practice. Person JSON-LD schema. Addresses the "who will look after me?" patient anxiety.
teds-team-pages/mei-wan.html
→ tedshealth.com/team/mei-wan/
Full profile: Registered Dietitian. Weight loss, metabolic health, type 2 diabetes remission. Positions a capability Arc TRT doesn't have. Target keywords: "metabolic health dietitian UK", "TRT weight loss".
teds-team-pages/trt-and-metabolic-health.html
→ tedshealth.com/trt-and-metabolic-health/
BONUS New content angle no competitor has. Explains the low-T + metabolic syndrome cycle, positions Ted's integrated model (TRT + dietitian), links to Mei Wan profile. Target: "TRT weight loss UK", "testosterone metabolism", "low testosterone type 2 diabetes". MedicalWebPage + FAQPage schema. Mei Wan is listed as author — boosts E-E-A-T significantly.
Implementation steps
1

Add these routes to your Webflow or hosting config P1

Create the URL structure: /team/, /team/dr-jonathan-andrews/, /team/ryann-egang/, /team/mei-wan/, /trt-and-metabolic-health/. In Webflow, add new pages and paste the HTML content, or use the embed widget if you're hosting static files.

2

Add Dr Andrews' GMC number P1

Currently the profile references GMC registration without a number. Displaying the actual GMC number is a meaningful trust signal — UK patients can verify instantly at gmc-uk.org. It takes one search to find it. Add it to the credentials line and JSON-LD schema ("identifier": {"@type": "PropertyValue", "name": "GMC", "value": "XXXXXXX"}).

3

Add profile photos P1

Currently using initials avatars (JA, RE, MW). Replace with actual headshots. Update the .avatar div to an <img> tag. Consistent professional headshots significantly increase the "real clinic" feel that converts TRT patients. Also add "image": "URL" to each Person/Physician JSON-LD schema block.

4

Link /team/ from the navbar and About Us page P1

The team hub needs inbound links to get indexed. Add it to: (1) the main nav under "About Us" section, (2) the /about-us page's team section — currently just text bios, link each name to their profile page.

5

Add the two unnamed doctors when you have them P2

The "what's new" blog mentions two additional doctors joined in 2025. Once you have their names and credentials, run python3 workspace/tools/generate_team_pages.py after adding their data to the TEAM list. The generator will produce all profiles in under a second.

6

Submit new URLs to Google Search Console P2

After publishing: go to Search Console > URL Inspection, paste each new URL, click "Request Indexing". Do this for all 5 new pages. Speeds up indexing from weeks to days.

7

Link /trt-and-metabolic-health/ from symptom pages and supplements P2

The metabolic health page is strong standalone SEO content. Link to it from: the weight-gain symptom page (if live), the /supplements/optimise page, and Mei Wan's profile (already done). Consider adding a "Metabolic Health" link to the main nav under treatments.

URL mapping
File Target URL Schema type Priority
teds-team-pages/index.html /team/ MedicalOrganization P1
teds-team-pages/dr-jonathan-andrews.html /team/dr-jonathan-andrews/ Physician P1
teds-team-pages/ryann-egang.html /team/ryann-egang/ Person P1
teds-team-pages/mei-wan.html /team/mei-wan/ Person P1
teds-team-pages/trt-and-metabolic-health.html /trt-and-metabolic-health/ MedicalWebPage + FAQPage BONUS
Why this matters — the context

E-E-A-T gap

Google's quality raters evaluate medical content on Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness, Trustworthiness (E-E-A-T). Named, credentialled clinician profiles with JSON-LD Person/Physician schema are one of the clearest signals. Arc TRT has 4 profiles. Ted's has 0. That's a meaningful ranking gap for any keyword where Google needs to decide which TRT clinic to trust.

Metabolic health is a genuine differentiator

Arc TRT doesn't have a dietitian. The /trt-and-metabolic-health/ page targets keyword clusters Arc CAN'T compete on without hiring one — "TRT weight loss UK", "testosterone metabolic syndrome", "low testosterone type 2 diabetes". Mei Wan is listed as the page author in JSON-LD, which is the right way to signal medical expertise for that content type.

Conversion benefit

TRT is a significant personal decision. Patients research their doctor before booking. Having no profile pages means every prospective patient hits a dead end when they try to answer "who will actually treat me?" Named profiles with credentials reduce this drop-off. The "same clinician every time" positioning becomes substantially more credible when you can SEE who that clinician is.

Updating or adding profiles

Re-generate any time

The generator script lives at workspace/tools/generate_team_pages.py. To add a new team member: add their entry to the TEAM list at the top of the file, then run python3 workspace/tools/generate_team_pages.py. All pages regenerate in under a second. Consistent style, schema, and linking guaranteed across all profiles.

Built by Nexus — AI Technical Lead, BGV. All pages are production-ready HTML. No external dependencies. No JavaScript frameworks. Fast-loading and crawler-friendly.
